name: elemental-shrine
summary: A shrine to an elemental power — fire, water, earth, air, or a blend. Shaped by the element itself: flame-blackened stone, ever-wet floor, growing roots, wind-carved openings. Pre-temple, primal, often in wilderness.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: varies by element — often irregular, shaped by the force it honors

accent.purpose: the element's mark — scorch, damp, moss, or carved spiral

primary_palette.note: fire shrine — eternal brazier circled by glassy shards
water shrine — a spring or well in a green-grown chamber
earth shrine — a monolith ringed by raw stone
air shrine — a whistling pillar and offerings of bone and feather
primal-energy shrine — a living crystal outcrop, warded but not tamed

story_prompts: a pilgrim mid-offering — weathered, road-stained, kneeling in the element
the element stirring — a rite has awakened it; the chamber is restless, smoke/water/wind abnormal
overgrown and half-reclaimed — the shrine has become part of the wilderness again
a recent sacrifice — blood or bone fresh among older layered offerings
a hermit-attendant has left — the fire neglected, the offerings undisturbed for days

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: elemental and irregular — firelit (0.4), sunlit through a cleft (0.5), or crystal-glow (0.3) depending on element

anti_patterns: formal symmetry feels wrong here — the element imposes its own logic
avoid pews, lecterns, or clerical furniture — this is pre-temple worship
the element MUST be visibly present — a fire, water, carved stone, rooted growth; not hinted
